SELECT value, isFinite(value) AS is_finite FROM test_finite;
SELECT value FROM test_finite WHERE isFinite(value) = 1;
SELECT value, CASE WHEN isFinite(value) = 1 THEN 'Finite' ELSE 'Not Finite' END AS status FROM test_finite;
SELECT AVG(value), isFinite(AVG(value)) AS avg_is_finite FROM test_finite;
SELECT isFinite(x) FROM (SELECT value AS x FROM test_finite) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE test_finite_joins (id UInt32, value Float64);
SELECT t1.value, t2.value, isFinite(t1.value) AS t1_is_finite, isFinite(t2.value) AS t2_is_finite FROM test_finite AS t1 JOIN test_finite_joins AS t2 ON t1.value > 0;
SELECT SUM(value), COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_finite WHERE isFinite(value) = 1) AS finite_values;
WITH (SELECT value FROM test_finite WHERE isFinite(value) = 0) AS non_finite_values SELECT COUNT(*) FROM non_finite_values;
SELECT value FROM test_finite WHERE isFinite(value) OR value = 0;
